name: irc-toolkit
documentation:
irc-toolkit
===========

An IRC client toolkit in Python.

## Installation

```bash
$ pip install irc-toolkit
```

## Usage

There are a few parts of irc-toolkit, depending on your goals.
[`irctk.Message`](https://irctk.readthedocs.io/en/latest/message.html)
offers IRC message parsing,
[`irctk.Client`](https://irctk.readthedocs.io/en/latest/client.html) offers an
IRC client which handles connection/channel/nick tracking giving you a callback
based interface for IRC messages, for example:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python

import asyncio
import logging

import irctk


class Bot:
    async def connect(self, hostname, port=6697, secure=True):
        client = irctk.Client()
        client.delegate = self
        await client.connect(hostname, port, secure)

    def irc_registered(self, client):
        client.send('MODE', client.nick, '+B')
        client.send('JOIN', '#test')

    def irc_private_message(self, client, nick, message):
        if message == 'ping':
            client.send('PRIVMSG', nick, 'pong')

    def irc_channel_message(self, client, nick, channel, message):
        if message == 'ping':
            client.send('PRIVMSG', channel, f'{nick}: pong')
        elif message == 'quit':
            client.quit()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Enable debug logging
    logging.basicConfig(level='DEBUG')

    bot = Bot()
    asyncio.run(bot.connect('irc.darkscience.net'))
```

methods.name: irctk.isupport.ISupport.parse
documentation: Parsing

methods.name: irctk.isupport.ISupport.maximum_nick_length
documentation:
Returns the maximum length of a nickname.

Example:

```
>>> support.maximum_nick_length
9
```
Get

methods.name: irctk.isupport.ISupport.maximum_channel_length
documentation:
Returns the maximum length of a channel name.

Example:

```
>>> support.maximum_channel_length
200
```

methods.name: irctk.isupport.ISupport.channel_prefixes
documentation:
Returns a list of channel prefixes.

Example:

```
>>> support.channel_prefixes
['#', '&']
```

methods.name: irctk.isupport.ISupport.case_mapping
documentation:
Returns the case mapping.

Example:

```
>>> support.case_mapping
'rfc1459'
```

methods.name: irctk.isupport.ISupport.is_channel
documentation:
Returns True if supplied channel name is a valid channel name.

Example:

```
>>> support.is_channel('#darkscience')
True

>>> support.is_channel('kylef')
False
```

methods.name: irctk.isupport.ISupport.bot_mode
documentation: https://ircv3.net/specs/extensions/bot-mode

classes.name: irctk.channel.Membership

methods.name: irctk.channel.Membership.has_mode
documentation:
Checks if the membership contains a user mode

```pycon
>>> membership.has_mode('o')
```
Represents a nick membership inside a channnel.

classes.name: irctk.channel.Channel

methods.name: irctk.channel.Channel.__str__
documentation: Channel name
Represents a channel

methods.name: irctk.message.Message.parse
documentation:
Parse an IRC line into a Message instance

```pycon
>>> message = Message.parse(':doe!doe@example.com PRIVMSG #example :Hello World')
>>> message.prefix
'doe!doe@example.com'
>>> message.command
'PRIVMSG'
>>> message.parameters
['#example', 'Hello World']
```

methods.name: irctk.message.Message.__str__
documentation:
Converts a Message instance into a string

```pycon
>>> message = Message(command='CAP', parameters=['LS'])
>>> str(message)
'CAP LS'
```

methods.name: irctk.message.Message.get
documentation:
Returns a parameter at index or None

```pycon
>>> message = Message.parse('JOIN #example')
>>> message.get(0)
'#example'
>>> message.get(1)
```

methods.name: irctk.message.Message.find_tag
documentation: Returns the first matching tag or None.
Message Tags

methods.name: irctk.message.Message.label
documentation:
Returns the label this message is in response to.

Requires labeled-responses capability
https://ircv3.net/specs/extensions/labeled-response to be enabled

methods.name: irctk.message.Message.batch
documentation:
Returns the batch of the message.

Requires batch capability
https://ircv3.net/specs/extensions/batch to be enabled

methods.name: irctk.message.Message.account
documentation:
Returns the account sending the message.

Requires account-tag capability
https://ircv3.net/specs/extensions/account-tag to be enabled

Disconnects from IRC and closes the connection. Accepts an optional
reason.
Socket

methods.name: irctk.client.Client.send_privmsg
documentation:
Sends a private message to a target.

Example:

```
>>> client.send_privmsg('kyle', 'Hi')
>>> client.send_privmsg(channel, 'Hi')
```

methods.name: irctk.client.Client.send_notice
documentation:
Sends a notice message to a target.

Example:

```
>>> client.send_notice('kyle', 'Hi')
>>> client.send_notice(channel, 'Hi')
```

methods.name: irctk.client.Client.send_join
documentation:
Sends a JOIN channel command.

    >>> client.send_join('#palaver')

methods.name: irctk.client.Client.send_part
documentation:
Sends a PART channel command.

    >>> client.send_part('#palaver')

methods.name: irctk.client.Client.send_line
documentation:
Sends a raw line to IRC

Example:

```
>>> client.send_line('PRIVMSG kylef :Hey!')
```

methods.name: irctk.client.Client.send
documentation:
Send an IRC message

```pycon
>>> client.send('JOIN', '#example')
```
